mnemonics.title: LIFO Reminder
mnemonics.content: Last In, First Out - Like a stack of plates: the last plate placed on top is the first one you'll take off.

mnemonics.title: Stack Operations Order
mnemonics.content: PUSH adds to top, POP removes from top - Think: Push Down, Pop Up - you push down onto stack, pop up from stack.

mnemonics.title: Push-Pop Flow
mnemonics.content: Push = put on top (increment top), Pop = take from top (decrement top), Peek = look at top (don't move).

acronyms.usage: Describes the fundamental principle of stack data structure
Used in queues (opposite of stack); helpful to remember difference
Postfix notation where operators follow operands; easily evaluated using stack
Prefix notation where operators precede operands; evaluated using stack from right to left

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical box (array) with bottom at index 0. Use a pointer arrow showing 'top' moving up/down with pushes and pops. Animate: push makes top arrow point higher, pop makes it point lower.
Imagine placing a book on a table - it goes on TOP. In diagram: draw new element floating above, arrow from top pointing to it, then arrow moves up to show new top.
Draw nodes as boxes with arrow pointing to next node. Head (top) is leftmost. Push adds new box to left with arrow pointing to old head. Pop removes leftmost box.
Draw left-to-right scanning line. When operand comes, push into stack (draw stack growing). When operator comes, draw curved arrows popping two values, show result pushed back.

analogies.analogy: Like Russian nesting dolls - each new doll (recursive call) goes inside the previous one, and you must open (return from) the innermost first to reach the outermost.
Every edit is 'pushed' onto a stack. When you press Ctrl+Z, it 'pops' the last edit to undo it - you can't undo the 5th edit before the 4th.
Your browsing history is a stack of URLs. The most recent page is on top; clicking 'back' pops it to go to the previous page.
Each peg is like a stack - you can only place disks on top. The smallest disk must be moved first (top of stack) to solve the puzzle.
